summary: Hezbollah launched 25 attacks on Israel between June 17‑18, firing 63 rockets, 1 missile, 7 UAVs and conducting other assaults (Source: @shin_persian/7128). Israel responded with intensive airstrikes in southern Lebanon, killing at least four Hezbollah fighters including Maj. Dor Gadlia and striking over 80 Hezbollah positions (Source: @amitsegal/62357; @inon_yttach/9065). Iran warned the Strait of Hormuz will stay closed until Israel withdraws from Lebanon and condemned Israel’s recent aggression in Lebanon (Source: @shin_persian/7129; @presstv/194791).
key_points: Hezbollah carried out 25 separate attacks on Israel from June 17‑18, employing rockets, a missile, UAVs, IEDs and a infiltration attempt (Source: @shin_persian/7128).
Israeli airstrikes in southern Lebanon killed four Hezbollah fighters, including Maj. Dor Gadlia, after a drone strike (Source: @amitsegal/62357; @lelotsenzura/93097).
IDF reported attacks on more than 80 Hezbollah command posts, launch sites and infrastructure in the Bakaa region and Nabatieh area (Source: @inon_yttach/9065).
Iran announced the Strait of Hormuz will remain closed until Israel fully withdraws from Lebanon and US forces leave the Persian Gulf (Source: @shin_persian/7129).
Iran’s Foreign Ministry condemned Israel’s latest “bloody aggression” against Lebanon, urging accountability for Washington (Source: @presstv/194791).
Jet activity was observed over Baghdad, indicating possible Iraqi air operations (Source: @shin_persian/7134).
Six suspects were identified in Syria near the Israeli border, though no security incident was reported (Source: @lelotsenzura/93091).

sections.executive_summary: Hezbollah intensified its campaign, executing 25 attacks on Israel with rockets, UAVs and a missile between June 17‑18. Israel answered with heavy airstrikes in southern Lebanon, killing at least four Hezbollah fighters and targeting over 80 positions. Iran escalated rhetoric, warning the Strait of Hormuz will stay closed until Israel withdraws from Lebanon and condemning Israeli actions in Lebanon.

points: Iran’s IRGC broadcast on maritime channel 16 that the Strait of Hormuz will remain closed until Israel fully withdraws from Lebanon and US forces leave the Persian Gulf, warning vessels to stay away (Source: @shin_persian/7129).
Iran’s Foreign Ministry issued a strong condemnation of Israel’s recent “bloody aggression” against Lebanon, calling for Washington’s accountability (Source: @presstv/194791).

Iranian statements linked the closure of the Hormuz Strait to the removal of the US “terrorist forces” from the region, framing it as a core condition for any US‑Iran agreement (Source: @shin_persian/7129).

Hezbollah leader Naim Qasim reaffirmed the group’s resolve, stating they will not surrender and have taken a “Karbalai” decision, indicating continued resistance against Israel (Source: @VahidOnline/76522).
The IDF announced strikes on Hezbollah infrastructure in the Bakaa area in response to repeated cease‑fire violations, targeting command posts and launch sites (Source: @idf_telegram/24389).

Israeli forces reported attacking more than 80 Hezbollah command posts, launch positions and additional infrastructure across southern Lebanon, reporting dozens of militant casualties (Source: @inon_yttach/9065).

Four Israeli soldiers, including Maj. Dor Gadlia, were killed by a Hezbollah‑launched drone in southern Lebanon, with three additional fighters reported dead (Source: @amitsegal/62357; @lelotsenzura/93097).
IDF statements indicated that over 100 targets were engaged during the night, with dozens of militants eliminated, and that operations will continue as needed (Source: @almogboker78/17521).
Two aerial targets were identified and engaged by Israeli air defenses, with no injuries reported (Source: @almogboker78/17525).
Hezbollah launched 25 attacks on Israel between June 17‑18, firing 63 rockets, a missile, 7 explosive UAVs and conducting IED attacks, despite a declared cease‑fire (Source: @shin_persian/7128).
Israeli airstrikes in southern Lebanon resulted in the death of four Hezbollah fighters, including Maj. Dor Gadlia, after a drone strike (Source: @amitsegal/62357; @lelotsenzura/93097).
IDF reported extensive strikes on Hezbollah positions in the Bakaa region and Nabatieh, targeting over 80 command posts and launch sites (Source: @inon_yttach/9065).
Hezbollah reportedly began targeting Israeli army leaders directly, indicating a shift to high‑value vehicle attacks (Source: @rasedal3ado138e/371687).
A senior Hezbollah commander warned that the group is prepared to strike Israeli leaders, emphasizing caution for Israeli forces (Source: @rasedal3ado138e/371687).
Six individuals were identified in Syria near the Israeli border, though authorities stated it was not a security incident (Source: @lelotsenzura/93091).
Jet activity was observed over Baghdad, suggesting possible Iraqi air operations or heightened aerial monitoring (Source: @shin_persian/7134).

key_developments: Hezbollah executed 25 attacks on Israel from June 17‑18, using rockets, a missile, UAVs and IEDs, indicating sustained offensive capability (Source: @shin_persian/7128).
Israeli airstrikes in southern Lebanon killed at least four Hezbollah fighters, including Maj. Dor Gadlia, and struck over 80 Hezbollah positions (Source: @amitsegal/62357; @inon_yttach/9065).
Iran declared the Strait of Hormuz will stay closed until Israel withdraws from Lebanon and US forces exit the Persian Gulf, warning vessels to avoid the area (Source: @shin_persian/7129).
Iran’s Foreign Ministry condemned Israel’s recent aggression in Lebanon, demanding accountability from Washington (Source: @presstv/194791).
IDF reported engagement of more than 100 targets in Lebanon, eliminating dozens of militants and continuing operations as needed (Source: @almogboker78/17521).
Six suspects were identified near the Israel‑Syria border, though no security incident was reported, highlighting ongoing intelligence activity (Source: @lelotsenzura/93091).
Jet activity over Baghdad was reported, suggesting possible Iraqi air operations or heightened regional aerial monitoring (Source: @shin_persian/7134).
sections.threat_assessment: Hezbollah maintains a high‑tempo attack posture against Israel, employing rockets, UAVs and infiltration teams, while Israeli forces intensify air campaigns in southern Lebanon, resulting in significant militant casualties. Iran’s strategic threat to close the Strait of Hormuz adds a maritime dimension that could disrupt regional trade and naval movements if Israel does not withdraw. The convergence of ground, aerial, and maritime threats raises the risk of broader escalation across the Levant and the Gulf.
sections.regional_response: Israel is conducting sustained strikes to degrade Hezbollah’s capabilities, while Iran leverages diplomatic and coercive tools, threatening the Hormuz Strait to pressure Israel and the United States. Hezbollah’s continued attacks despite a cease‑fire suggest limited influence of diplomatic channels, and the identification of suspects near the Syrian border indicates ongoing intelligence cooperation among regional actors. Gulf states remain observers, with no direct involvement reported in the current data set.
sections.intelligence_notes: Monitoring of Hezbollah’s rocket and UAV launch sites should be prioritized, as recent patterns show rapid deployment across multiple fronts. Iranian maritime warnings require close tracking of commercial vessel movements through Hormuz to anticipate potential closures. Coordination between Israeli air assets and ground intelligence is essential to sustain pressure on Hezbollah while minimizing civilian exposure. Continued surveillance of Syrian border activity is advised to detect any spillover of militant movements.
